metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The Art of Statistics traces how a claim, like moderate alcohol protecting health, can break down at each stage of reasoning. Early stages face measurement problems, such as whether people truthfully report their own drinking. Later stages face external validity, since excluding institutionalized people distorts what the registry association really shows. Different displays reveal different truths: strip charts, box plots, and histograms expose distinct patterns, and standard deviation misleads on skewed data.

artifactLines: The Art of Statistics traces how a claim, like moderate alcohol protecting health, can break down at each stage of reasoning.
Early stages face measurement problems, such as whether people truthfully report their own drinking.
Later stages face external validity, since excluding institutionalized people distorts what the registry association really shows.
Different displays reveal different truths: strip charts, box plots, and histograms expose distinct patterns, and standard deviation misleads on skewed data.

beats.narration_text: The staged critique of the moderate-alcohol question, moving from measurement problems to external validity because institutionalized people are excluded, showed me how one association can mislead at every step. The point that strip charts, box plots, and histograms each reveal different patterns is what I want to practice. Can you (1) walk the alcohol claim through all four stages naming the specific bias at each, (2) explain why standard deviation misleads on skewed data where the median holds, and (3) pick which chart I should use to expose the hidden confounder?
